H1项目奖.project_name: SEA E-commerce Ads TR Improvement Joint-Project
H1项目奖.reason:
The e-commerce and monetisation teams focused on separate goals—GMV growth vs. revenue and Take Rate (TR). To unlock sustainable growth, the SEA E-commerce Ads TR Improvement Project aligned both objectives into a self-reinforcing cycle: grow revenue, reinvest in users and merchants, boost GMV, and repeat.
This was achieved through two major efforts: embedding ad value into the organic distribution system and fostering close cross-team collaboration. By introducing eCPM-based interventions across all traffic endpoints—from search to feeds—the project optimized recommendation logic for both user experience and platform profit. As a result, ad TR rose from 5.5% to 7.3%, while GMAX’s GMV share jumped from 32% to 49%, and income share from 70% to 88%—the fastest TR growth seen across all e-commerce platforms.
Importantly, this was done without compromising GMV. In reverse experiments, ads ADVV increased by 14%, proving value-added traffic efficiency.
The project also laid the groundwork for long-term system unification: organic and ads systems are now converging with shared value signals, cutting redundant development and data costs.
A “shared-metrics” culture underpins this success. The organic team now owns profitability as a KPI, while the ads team incorporates GMV and user experience into its goals. Advertising has evolved into a core business lever—integrated into merchant tools, creator incentives, payment systems, and even AM performance evaluations. OKRs across functions are fully aligned.
This project integrated monetisation and growth into one unified engine driving platform success.

Advertising has evolved into a core business lever—integrated into merchant tools, creator incentives, payment systems, and even AM performance evaluations. OKRs across functions are fully aligned.
This project integrated monetisation and growth into one unified engine driving platform success.

The COD project team drove notable GMV & Profit growth across multiple countries:
1. Multi-Country Business Growth: Customized logistics algorithm optimizations delivered GMV increases of 2.9%~7.2% across markets, with SEA annual profit rising by $63.3 million.  
2. ID best practise: Full rollout of COD (Cash on Delivery) payments boosted Indonesi's GMV +7.2%, order+ 7.3%, and DNC +83%, contributing $25M in annual profit.  
3, Regional Scaling: The ID model was successfully replicated in Thailand and Malaysia, generating an estimated $17 million and $11 million in annual profit, respectively, with an average GMV uplift of ~2.9%. 
Cross-Functional Collaboration
This success was made possible through seamless collaboration among the logistics algorithm, product R&D, UG, asset management, DS, finance, and country operations teams, ensuring rapid execution and sustained business impact.

The project team optimized subsidy efficiency and co-funding strategy from a macro perspective, contributing to GMV and revenue growth 
- **Improved subsidy visibility via platform-wide experiments: **launch A/B experiments beyond UG in H1 2025, aligning ROI measurement across major budget categories. A/B test coverage expanded from 50% to 85%, resolving inconsistencies in ROI standards.
- **Designed rigorous experiments to isolate ROI drivers:** used holdout experiments to separate strategy-driven ROI uplift from natural market growth. Leveraged additive experiments to quantify the incremental impact of combining tools (e.g., free shipping), supporting budget allocation and marginal return analysis.
- **UG-level ROI optimization:** Through co-funding programs (higher take rate + improved penetration) and centralized subsidy strategies, the ROI improved by ~0.1–0.4 across all markets vs. Dec 2024.
- **Scaled platform growth via manual + algorithmic optimization: **Subsidies contributed a 5.5% GMV uplift in A/B tests—1.2pt from additional funding and 4.3pt from strategic optimization.
- **Drove revenue growth through co-funding programs: **By May, platform take rate (TR) saw strong improvements vs. Dec 2024:ID +0.9pt, VN +1.9pt, TH +0.8pt, PH +1.4pt, MY +2.4pt, SG +0.8pt.

H2项目奖.project_name: Cross-border Compliance Risk Management
H2项目奖.reason:
In 2025 H2, SEA cross-border team faced significant regulatory and policy shifts across multiple markets, creating cost pressure and uncertainty for merchants.
In response, the business team partnered with Logistics, GR, Product, GNE, and other cross-functional teams to form a dedicated taskforce. Together, they quickly identified risks and opportunities, implemented systematic solutions, and supported merchants in adapting their operating models — stabilizing the foundation of the business at a critical moment.
Finally, market share across multiple countries increased by 10–30 percentage points during the period of regulatory change. Beyond immediate results, the team also strengthened product capabilities and built scalable compliance response mechanisms for the future.
Through shared ownership, rapid decision-making, and true boundaryless collaboration, this team set a powerful example of what cross-functional partnership can achieve.

H2项目奖.project_name: Philippines Outer Islands Initiative – Volume-for-Price Strategy
H2项目奖.reason:
This project was jointly driven by the Philippines Operations, regional Logistics, User Growth, and Finance teams, focusing on the outer island markets of Visayas and Mindanao.
- To ensure sustainable growth, the Philippines Operations led cross-functional collaboration with Logistics, UG, and Finance to develop a negotiation strategy with logistics partner J&T. The approach centered on securing cost reductions in exchange for committed order volume. This resulted in a special pricing agreement offering discounts on incremental orders in the outer islands.
- In 2025 Q4, UG and Ops teams initiated experiments to increase shipping subsidies in the developing regions, achieving a significant uplift in GMV (+3.1%) and MAC (+4.6%), albeit with a cost increase of 1pt only. Based on this logic, PH Ops organized XFN to collaborate closely and planned negotiation strategy together with Logistic team.
- Right after logistic team successfully drove and concluded "volume-for-price" strategy negotiations with their great expertees, UG team run out a zero-minimum-spend free shipping strategy. This initiative fueled a +25% WoW increase in outer islands orders, significantly strengthening TTS's market position in the developing regions. In December, costs were diluted significantly, and at the same time the initiative drove strong growth in both GMV and MAC. GMV control share +13.2pt MoM, and MAC control share +9pt MoM, both hitting historical highs.

H2项目奖.project_name: SEA Next Day Delivery Expansion Project
H2项目奖.reason:
**[Project Results]**
Fast delivery experience has become a key service capability for experience competition among multiple platforms, which can help platforms improve pre-purchase conversion and post-purchase satisfaction.
Since January 2025, SEA's NDD penetration rate has increased by 6.5pt to 2x%, and the display rate of NDD tags has increased by 1x times to 2x%. Through A/B test verification, displaying a high proportion of NDD tags throughout the buyers' entire shopping lifecycle can drive pre-purchase conversion ***(Malaysia per capita GMV +0.47%, Indonesia per capita GMV +0.24%)***. Without significantly increasing costs, the NDD penetration rate has surpassed or aligned with competitors in each country.
Fast delivery experience has become a key service capability for multi-platforms in the competition of logistics experience, which can help platforms improve pre-purchase conversion and post-purchase satisfaction.
**[Collaboration Highlights]**
Organized by the Logistics Team and collaborated with GNE, Industry, Logistics Operations, Product, Algorithm, R&D and IMC teams to clarify each party’s key objectives.
- **Optimize Policies & Tools for Seller Shipping**
Since H2 2025, NDD service has been expanded to all sellers. Via the new NDDR indicator, Seller Center notifications and NDD order priority sorting, we increased the same-day shipping rate of NDD orders by 5.6pt.
- **Clarify NDD Routes & Optimize Next-day delivery rate**
Logs team defined NDD routes with logistics providers, addressed all bottlenecks, and improved the next-day delivery rate by 4pt through more collection waves, streamlined transit routes and independent NDD packaging.
- **Enhance Buyer Perception via Tag Display & Marketing**
Boosting buyers' awareness of NDD by adding labels in all fields and using algorithms for dynamic display. We also launched twice UX marketing campaigns with IMC and logistics providers, achieving 10 million exposures to build long-term fast delivery awareness.
- **Periodic Reviews for Strategy Implementation**
With clear responsibilities for each team, weekly XFN meetings aligned progress. Through 4-time strategy revolution, we improved NDD performance.
H2项目奖.department: Supply Chain and Logistics-SEA Logistics Operations

Through A/B test verification, displaying a high proportion of NDD tags throughout the buyers' entire shopping lifecycle can drive pre-purchase conversion ***(Malaysia per capita GMV +0.47%, Indonesia per capita GMV +0.24%)***. Without significantly increasing costs, the NDD penetration rate has surpassed or aligned with competitors in each country.
Fast delivery experience has become a key service capability for multi-platforms in the competition of logistics experience, which can help platforms improve pre-purchase conversion and post-purchase satisfaction.
**[Collaboration Highlights]**
Organized by the Logistics Team and collaborated with GNE, Industry, Logistics Operations, Product, Algorithm, R&D and IMC teams to clarify each party’s key objectives.
- **Optimize Policies & Tools for Seller Shipping**
Since H2 2025, NDD service has been expanded to all sellers. Via the new NDDR indicator, Seller Center notifications and NDD order priority sorting, we increased the same-day shipping rate of NDD orders by 5.6pt.
- **Clarify NDD Routes & Optimize Next-day delivery rate**
Logs team defined NDD routes with logistics providers, addressed all bottlenecks, and improved the next-day delivery rate by 4pt through more collection waves, streamlined transit routes and independent NDD packaging.
- **Enhance Buyer Perception via Tag Display & Marketing**
Boosting buyers' awareness of NDD by adding labels in all fields and using algorithms for dynamic display. We also launched twice UX marketing campaigns with IMC and logistics providers, achieving 10 million exposures to build long-term fast delivery awareness.
- **Periodic Reviews for Strategy Implementation**
With clear responsibilities for each team, weekly XFN meetings aligned progress. Through 4-time strategy revolution, we improved NDD performance.

H2项目奖.project_name: SEA BNPL Penetration Scale Up
H2项目奖.reason:
Driven by the strategic synergy between TTS SEA and PIPO (Global Payment) in 2025, the teams successfully achieved comprehensive coverage across all five Southeast Asian markets. Through initiatives such as co-funding with TTS Ops, optimizing checkout flow and adding entry points with TTS Product team, the cross-functional effort further improved BNPL penetration.
Meanwhile, by transcending the limitations of the white-label model and pivoting to a Self-Operated + Risk-Sharing framework, we have achieved deeper control over user experience and risk management strategies. This paradigm combined with the deep collaboration between TTS and PIPO propelled the regional penetration rate from 3.07% (end of '24) to 6.32% (+122.48%), significantly outperforming our annual targets.

Driven by the strategic synergy between TTS SEA and PIPO (Global Payment) in 2025, the teams successfully achieved comprehensive coverage across all five Southeast Asian markets. Through initiatives such as co-funding with TTS Ops, optimizing checkout flow and adding entry points with TTS Product team, the cross-functional effort further improved BNPL penetration.
Meanwhile, by transcending the limitations of the white-label model and pivoting to a Self-Operated + Risk-Sharing framework, we have achieved deeper control over user experience and risk management strategies. This paradigm combined with the deep collaboration between TTS and PIPO propelled the regional penetration rate from 3.07% (end of '24) to 6.32% (+122.48%), significantly outperforming our annual targets.

Casey is the BMKT POC within CB IMC campaigns team, responsible for shaping the seller communications strategy for cross-border sellers. With the core objective of increasing CB seller participation during campaigns, Casey proactively explored innovative approaches beyond conventional marketing channels. He identified seller missions as a scalable channel for seller communications and, despite this being outside of marketing’s typical scope, took the initiative to deeply understand the seller missions platform. Casey independently tested mission and voucher setups and incorporated this capability into his end-to-end marketing plan. He led the design of the CB seller mission strategy, developed the associated budget and ROI projections, and worked closely with cross-functional stakeholders—including BARM, Finance, and local markets—to drive execution. The 12.12 pilot delivered strong results: total CB seller participation increased by 34% vs 11.11, with seller missions contributing 64K participants, contributing to ~10% of total seller participation. The success of this trial validated seller missions as an effective incentive when paired with sustained seller communications, and the mechanic has since been earmarked for continuation in future SS-tier campaigns.

Background and Objective - The go-live rate of KLS Exclusive Seller, one of the core live sellers for TH, significantly dropped during 8.8 - With the low go-live rate, the live GMV per sellers was below $4,000 while contributed below 40% of campaign daily live GMV  3. Solution KLS Live Battle was first introduced during 9.9 and further optimized during 11.11. The mechanics to incentivize sellers was divided into 2 tracks and public recognition 1) Track 1: Seller Live GMV target --- Milestone 1: achieve 120% completion of daily live target, incentivized up to $150 via D+1 live vouchers --- Milestone 2 : achieve 150% completion of daily live target, incentivized up to $350 via D+1 live vouchers 2) Track 2: Incentivize $7,500 per cluster when achieving more than 90% go-live rate 3) Public recognition via Trophy and Social Media post: --- Trophy: Top 3 highest Self-Live sellers in 1) Mall sellers for each cluster, 2) Non-Mall sellers for each cluster --- Social Media Post: Reward by each sub-category under each cluster  4. Impact The KLS Live Battle has significantly incentivized sellers to go live, resulting in - Achieved 99.4% go-live rate during 11.11, only Lifestyle under achieved 2% go-live while FMCG, Fashion and EL all achieved 100% go-live rate - Contributed 41% of daily live GMV, or 13% of platform DAGMV --- KLS Exclusive Sellers daily live GMV uplifted 52% (11.11 vs 10.10) --- Overall platform live GMV grew 34% (11.11 vs 10.10) --- GMV per sellers also created historical highest $4.7K per seller
